course_description:
This course will allow students to build an intuitive understanding of the key financial concepts, tools and frameworks to better communicate with key stakeholders to further your career. Emphasis is placed on placing rigour in an impactful and practical context to analyse financial problems and devise solutions. Real world and real-time examples of events and developments are analysed and interwoven throughout the course to enhance skills relevant to managing financial problems in challenging environments.

Course Changes in Response to Previous Student Feedback

In response to previous student feedback, we’ve updated the course to include more real-world examples (with guest speakers, videos, and case studies), put less emphasis on portfolio theory and more on practical options and derivatives, lean a bit more toward interpretation than heavy maths, and provide more feedback. We’ve also tightened up the overlap between Assessment 1 and Assessment 2, kept more of the scenario-style approach students preferred in Assessment 3, broadened the focus beyond just the investor perspective, and (from the MBA academic session onward) added short recaps of earlier concepts before moving into new content.

course_aims: This course aims to provide students with a set of mathematical, statistical and analytical tools that will enable them to value financial securities, construct portfolios of financial securities, and determine the price impact that a proposed corporate investment will have on shareholder value.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an understanding of the key global influences and trends shaping the investment management industry.
Analyse problems to formulate and defend solutions, to achieve financial goals and objectives.
Apply core financial principles and investment management techniques to real world examples and experience.
Comprehend the principles of risk within the realm of finance to evaluate and recommend different strategies for mitigating these risks.
In teams, complete a portfolio analysis based on available financial data.

assessment_summary.title: A1: Oral Presentation
assessment_summary.due_date: Slides & Speaker Notes 20/03/2026 5:00 pm; Review Board Materials 27/03/2026 5:00 pm; Live Presentation and Q&A - During Class 1/04/2026 5:30 pm; Oral presentation is either delivered in Week 6 lecture or tutorial. Week 4, 5, 6 dates are subject to change.

assessment_summary.title: A3: Real-World Scenario and Financial Problem Report

assessment_details.title: A1: Oral Presentation
assessment_details.due_date: Slides & Speaker Notes 20/03/2026 5:00 pm Review Board Materials 27/03/2026 5:00 pm Live Presentation and Q&A - During Class 1/04/2026 5:30 pm Oral presentation is either delivered in Week 6 lecture or tutorial. Week 4, 5, 6 dates are subject to change.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Peer assessed. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The oral presentation consists of a 5 minute presentation followed by 5 minutes of Q&A, which will be held in class. You will be assessed on your: (i) written memorandum (i.e., a summary of your financial analysis), (ii) ability to present your key findings, and (iii) ability to answer questions from the audiences. If you are unable to attend the oral presentation, you must contact the course coordinator. Otherwise, penalties will be applied accordingly. Further details about oral presentation will be discussed in class and posted to our course Blackboard site. Please Note: The presentation will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy AI Statement Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 7 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
An extension may be available for the submitted material only. Please contact the Course Coordinator if you are unable to attend your presentation.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
10% Late Penalty applies to submitted material only. Late submissions are not accepted for in-class presentations. Failure to present at the scheduled time will result in a mark of zero for the presentation portion of this assessment.

assessment_details.title: A2: Financial Evaluation
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessed. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: For this project, you will be in a team of 4 or 5 members. Your task will be to undertake security valuation and portfolio analysis. Full details, and relevant materials will be provided on the course Blackboard site and discussed in class. Peer Review: Group members are expected to contribute equally, fulfilling the tasks agreed on. If there are disagreements in relation to team members' contributions that cannot be resolved, students must bring the problem to the attention of their Course Coordinator as early as possible. Students can elect to undertake a Peer Review process, which can influence the marks of those who do not contribute to group work. AI Statement Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: A3: Real-World Scenario and Financial Problem Report
assessment_details.task_description: In this assessment, students will be given a scenario(s) and are asked to identify a real-world financial problem that pertains to the prescribed topic(s) as indicated on the task description page on Blackboard. The scenario(s) and selected financial problem should be current, relevant, and associated with a specific firm, industry, or market. The task involves analysing the problem using financial theories and models covered in-class and providing practical recommendations for resolving the issue. You will be assessed on the accuracy and clarity of answers. More details will be provided on Blackboard prior to the assessment release date. AI STATEMENT Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
